creator: ganael.laplanche@martymac.com
text:
On Wed, 29 Apr 2009 09:17:06 -0400, Steven Kreuzer wrote

Hello Steven,

> The distfile that is hosted on sourceforge and jenkinssoftware.com  
> have different checksums. I grabbed a copy of both and compared them
> and it seems like while the versions are correct, the distfile from  
> sourceforge has additional files that are not in distfile provided by  
> jenkinssoftware.com
> In addition, the checksums of certian files differ as well.
> 
> Cn you please figure out which is the "correct" distfile to use?

I was aware of that problem, and I have already emailed the developper to ask
him to put exactly the same files for future versions (I hope he will do so).

For that particular port version, checksums I used are the ones from
jenkinssoftware.com, not sourceforge. But I left sourceforge listed in the
MASTER_SITES for future versions of the port (and also to make the port being
able to use sourceforge if the developper decided to upload the 'good' version
of the software to SF again).

> Also, it is not necessary to gzip and uuencode patches. Simply  
> submitting plaintext unified diffs is very much appreciated. Thanks

Oh, sorry. I often sumbit PRs as plain text, but for big patches, I've already
got bitten and had to gzip them. So sometimes, I send big patches gzipped
without trying plain text first :p

Best regards,

Ganaël LAPLANCHE
ganael.laplanche@martymac.com
http://www.martymac.com
